content:
Given the undirected dynamic graph with the edges:
[(3, 6, 3), (6, 7, 3), (1, 2, 5), (2, 4, 5), (3, 7, 6), (6, 9, 6), (1, 8, 9), (4, 6, 9), (3, 5, 11), (6, 8, 11), (1, 6, 12), (5, 7, 13), (8, 9, 14), (1, 3, 16), (2, 5, 18)],

We need to find the nodes linked with node 7 exactly at time 3.

After scanning each edge, we can collect the other node whenever the time t matches the queried time AND one of the nodes matches the queried node.

Here are the nodes linked with node 7 at time 3:

* Edge (3, 6, 3) has node 7 and time 3. -> Add node 6.
* Edge (6, 7, 3) has node 7 and time 3. -> Add node 6.
* Edge (1, 2, 5) has node 7 but time is 5 (not 3). -> Ignore.
* Edge (2, 4, 5) has node 7 but time is 5 (not 3). -> Ignore.
* Edge (3, 7, 6) has node 7 and time 3. -> Add node 3.
* Edge (6, 9, 6) has node 7 but time is 6 (not 3). -> Ignore.
* Edge (1, 8, 9) has node 7 but time is 9 (not 3). -> Ignore.
* Edge (4, 6, 9) has node 7 but time is 9 (not 3). -> Ignore.
* Edge (3, 5, 11) has node 7 but time is 11 (not 3). -> Ignore.
* Edge (6, 8, 11) has node 7 but time is 11 (not 3). -> Ignore.
* Edge (1, 6, 12) has node 7 but time is 12 (not 3). -> Ignore.
* Edge (5, 7, 13) has node 7 and time 3. -> Add node 5.
* Edge (8, 9, 14) has node 7 but time is 14 (not 3). -> Ignore.
* Edge (1, 3, 16) has node 7 but time is 16 (not 3). -> Ignore.
* Edge (2, 5, 18) has node 7 and time 3. -> Add node 2.

The nodes matched are 6, 3, and 5.

Answer: [6, 3, 5]
